briefing.headline: Rig up a Carolina Rig — steady spawn approach for stained water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Carolina Rig is a strong choice for spawn with stained water and stable conditions.

Wheeler: Rigs a curly-tail worm (cut paddle tail) on a Carolina rig with 3/4-oz egg sinker, 22-inch fluorocarbon leader (range 18-26 inches), and 3/0 worm hook; retrieves with standard pull-and-drag devoid of deadstick pauses.

Key adjustment for stable: moderate cadence — match the natural pace of local forage.

primaryApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: Rigs a curly-tail worm (cut paddle tail) on a Carolina rig with 3/4-oz egg sinker, 22-inch fluorocarbon leader (range 18-26 inches), and 3/0 worm hook; retrieves with standard pull-and-drag d
primaryApproach.targets: Focus on flat, grass, dock. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: Yamamoto pro Yelas: 'Right around the spawn and after, the fish seem to prefer a smaller bait with less action' — downsize from the Cowboy during spawn and post-spawn
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on flat, grass, dock. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: Yamamoto pro Fukae: The Free Rig is a cross between a Texas Rig and Carolina Rig — the line runs freely through the weight's eye so the weight falls first and the soft plastic flutters down behind it with an unpredictable slow fall; use a 'lift an...

KVD: 'Don't assume you must finesse spawning bass. At the 2011 Classic, 4 of top 7 finishers power fished spinnerbaits and crankbaits on the same 150-yard spawning flat. Key conditions: hard bottom flat with stumps + grass, 10-degree water temp ri...
Johnston: Garmin Perspective Mode is critical for bed fishing in low-visibility conditions. Relied heavily on it along with the Power-Pole Move trolling motor for targeting bed fish.

briefing.depthStrategy: Stable spawn conditions have fish committed to beds in 1-5ft. Focus on hard-bottom areas in protected coves, pockets, and flats. Polarized glasses are essential for sight-fishing.
adjustIf: If conditions suddenly shift (pressure drop, wind change), be ready to speed up your approach — transitional moments produce big bites
If water clears significantly, downsize presentations and move to natural color patterns
If bites dry up, try shaking in place for 10-15 seconds before moving — patience pays with plastics
